title: Lithospermum johnstonii
intro: Lithospermum johnstonii, also known as Johnston's gromwell, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and improve wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum johnstonii include lithospermoside, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical ointments and creams for skin and wound care.
taxonomy: Lithospermum johnstonii is classified within the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to Africa, with Lithospermum johnstonii being a specific species. The classification of Lithospermum johnstonii is further divided into the order Boraginales, a group of flowering plants that include borage and forget-me-not species. Within the Lithospermeae tribe, Lithospermum johnstonii is a distinct species with its own unique characteristics.
